exemplar.title: How can the relationship between aperture, shutter speed, and ISO be modelled mathematically to optimise exposure, depth of field and minimise motion blur, graininess in various scenarios?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can the relationship between aperture, shutter speed, and ISO be modelled mathematically to optimise exposure, depth of field and minimise motion blur, graininess in various scenarios?

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear research question providing strong focus (Page 1)
Basic structure with headings, table of contents and consistent section labels
weaknesses: Misplaced or empty subsections and pages out of numerical order hamper navigation
Lengthy blocks of text and repetition (e.g., EV formula appears multiple times) reduce conciseness
Inconsistent formatting of tables and captions disrupts the reading flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Presentation were assessed at band 2. The exploration has an adequate organization and recognisable logical sequence, but uneven coherence (abrupt jumps) and superfluous content (repetition, long anecdotes) prevent it reaching band 3. Thus a score of 2/4 accurately reflects its overall performance.

strengths: Use of multiple representations (tables, scatter and line graphs) to support modelling (Page 9)
Application of the EV-based shutter-speed model back to primary data shows internal consistency
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation and missing variable definitions (e.g., aperture formula inline without legend)
Graphs and plots lack complete axis labels and units, and some tables contain empty cells
Long algebraic steps with limited accompanying commentary limit clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand in Mathematical Communication was placed in band 2: some appropriate language and notation but inconsistent, moderate use of multiple representations, and partial logical clarity. A score of 2/4 best represents this moderate but flawed execution.

strengths: Systematic planning shown by a clear bullet list of investigative steps (Page 5)
Personal motivation clearly linked to family photography context
weaknesses: Methodology follows a typical IA pattern, limiting distinctiveness
Weightings for different conditions are chosen subjectively without external justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Personal Engagement received band 2: significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach with good creativity, but not the originality or innovation to merit band 3. A score of 2/3 reflects this solid yet conventional engagement.

strengths: Interpretation ties model outcomes to expert photographic advice effectively (Page 22)
weaknesses: Analysis of results is largely descriptive with no error or sensitivity discussion
Evaluation of outcomes is limited to a brief strengthsâlimitations list and lacks future implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is some meaningful reflection, deeper critical analysis and comprehensive evaluation are lacking (two strands at band 1). Therefore an overall Reflection score of 1/3 aligns with the limited critique and minimal evaluation demonstrated.

strengths: Relevant mathematics (logarithms, linear regression, normalization, composite cost functions) applied appropriately in context
Level of mathematics (algebraic manipulation, statistical modelling) is clearly commensurate with SL
weaknesses: Numerical inconsistencies and unit errors in calculations (e.g., brightness target justification)
Omission of intermediate statistical values and incomplete presentation of normalization steps reduce transparency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics strands were assessed at bands 3, 3 and 2. The work uses relevant SL-level mathematics appropriately but contains errors and omissions that prevent a higher band. A score of 3/6 fairly represents this competent yet imperfect mathematical application.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations generally accompany calculations, but long algebraic steps are copied without commentary and transitions between ideas are abrupt, limiting clarity. Logical structure is therefore partial rather than good, warranting band 2.

The student generally understands formulas and carries them through correctly, yet several numerical inconsistencies (e.g., brightness target justification, duplicated slope values, unit errors) reveal only partial accuracy. Hence âsome understanding with errorsâ (band 2) best describes performance.

The student supplies tables, scatter/line graphs and one piecewise sketch, but many graphs lack labelled axes or units and some images are barely legible. Diversity of representation exists, though effectiveness is only moderate; hence band 2 fits.

The report follows a recognisable sequence (introduction, data collection, modelling, discussion) and the narrative usually moves forward logically; however, frequent digressions, repeated explanations, and occasional abrupt jumps (e.g. between EV and brightness models) break the flow. Overall coherence is present but uneven, fitting the descriptor for âsome coherence.â

Creating piecewise cost functions and attempting optimisation show creativity and initiative above a minimal level, yet the mathematical tools remain elementary and the implementation contains many flaws; thus a âgoodâ (band 2) level is appropriate.

Analysis of results is largely descriptive; the student restates that ISO 1600 is recommended and claims the model is âaccurateâ without error estimates or sensitivity checks. This limited critique aligns with band 1.

Evaluation is confined to a brief strengths-and-limitations list and a note that 1600 ISO âaligns with expertsâ. There is little discussion of mathematical validity or future work, so only a limited evaluation is demonstrated (band 1).

The student reflects on how the minimum of the cost curve matches photographic advice and recognises assumptions (camera uniformity), providing meaningful reflection throughout, but depth is not âsubstantialâ. Band 2 matches these qualities.

Symbols (A, t, EV, sgn) and logarithmic notation are used, yet typesetting is inconsistent (mixed italics/roman, missing parentheses, misuse of âflashâ for f-stop). Errors are not overwhelming but prevent âmostly consistentâ accuracy, so band 2 is appropriate.

The topic is clearly personal (family photography) and choices such as weighting coefficients reveal ownership. Nevertheless, methodology follows a fairly typical IA pattern, so evidence of a distinctive approach is solid but not âhighly personal.â

Considerable repetition (EV formula appears three times, linear-regression steps written twice) and inclusion of lengthy anecdotal rationale add superfluous content. Most material is relevant, but conciseness is only âmostlyâ achieved, matching band 2.

Logarithms, proportional reasoning, linear regression, normalisation, and a composite cost model are all relevant to the problem and are applied in context. Although execution is imperfect, the mathematics is undeniably relevant and used appropriately, satisfying band 3.

Designing a bespoke cost function and gathering original data demonstrate noticeable independent thought, yet heavy reliance on standard linear regression and textbook EV material shows the thinking is not outstanding; band 2 is justified.

Headings, a table of contents, and consistent section labels show basic structure, yet several subsections are misplaced or empty, pages are out of numerical order, and long blocks of text hamper navigation. Organisation is therefore adequate but not âclearâ enough for band 3.

The work sits comfortably at SL level: algebraic manipulation, base-2 logarithms, statistical measures and graphical modelling. While not sophisticated enough for higher bands, it is certainly âcommensurate with course levelâ, indicating band 3.

annotations.comment: The transition into optimization appears abruptly; consider summarizing prior steps before defining cost functions.
The shutterâspeed threshold $$t_0$$ appears before a clear definition; introduce its context and recommended values earlier.
Table 2 is referenced but no descriptive title is provided, and its placement disrupts narrative flow.
Regression parameters for depth of field are correctly derived, but intermediate summation values (Î£x, Î£y, Î£xy) are omitted; include for transparency.
Regression for graininess excludes ISO <1600 without statistical justification; discuss impact on model validity.
The research question is clearly stated and directly links to the context of photography optimization, providing a strong focus for the exploration.
The questionâs broad scope may lead to overcomplexity; consider narrowing to a specific environment or fixed independent variable to deepen analysis.
Table of contents shows consistent formatting, but missing page numbers in key sections reduce navigability and clarity.
The introduction articulates personal motivation well, but breaking this long paragraph into shorter sections would improve readability and focus.
The formula for aperture diameter is stated inline but lacks explicit variable definitions and units; include a legend for clarity.
Listing the investigative steps in a clear bullet list demonstrates systematic planning and enhances logical development.
Dependent variables are mentioned but not explicitly listed in a table or enumeration; formalise these definitions for clarity.
The large data table on shutter speeds contains several empty cells and inconsistent notation, hindering data interpretation.
Deriving the proportional relationship $$t\propto\frac1{f^2}$$ is accurate and effectively supports later modeling.
Graphs lack axis labels and units, which obscures interpretation; ensure each figure has complete annotations.
The EV values calculated in Table 1 have small fluctuations but no error discussion; reflect on possible measurement errors.
The caption âTable 1 â effect of an increasing aperture...â is formatted as a section header; use consistent table numbering and styling.
Applying the EV-based shutter speed model back to primary data shows consistency; good use of multiple representations.
The Desmos plot lacks axis labels and scale markers, which limits its effectiveness as a mathematical representation.
Normalization of graininess yields negative values; explain the choice of mean-subtraction and scaling to avoid misinterpretation.
Header âProcessed and normalised graninessâ is misspelled and variable spelled inconsistently; revise for accuracy.
Motion blur cost function involves multiple nested parentheses and sgn; break into separate sub-expressions for better comprehension.
The strengths and limitations section lists points but provides limited future research; suggest defining precise next steps or experiments.
Citation styles vary across entries; standardize formatting (e.g., MLA or APA) for consistency and academic rigor.
Defining brightness cost uses a computed target brightness of 1.95, yet numeric steps are omitted; include substitution for clarity.
The graininessâcost equation on page 17 omits a closing parenthesis before the weight term; correct syntax to avoid misinterpretation.
Normalization formulas include brightness and graininess but omit motion blur and depth of field; present all normalization steps together.
Weightings for street and night photography are chosen subjectively; strengthen by referencing specific expert recommendations or studies.
The final costâfunction expression on page 20 is dense; consider splitting into numbered steps or using a flowchart for clarity.
Costâfunction graph lacks annotated axis labels and does not mark the optimal minimum point explicitly; update the plot for precision.
The interpretation ties results to expert literature effectively, reflecting on how ISO 1600 aligns with photography advice.
